subject: Chemistry
solution:
To calculate the mass percent of ''X'' in the solution, we need to determine the mass of ''X'' and the total mass of the solution.

Given:

:Solute (X) = 2 g

Solvent (H2O) = 1 mole = 18 g

Total mass = 2 + 18 = 20 g

 Mass percent of X=22+18×100=10

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The combustion reaction can be represented as 

CxHy+x+y4O2→xCO2+yH2O

At STP, 1 mole of any gas occupies 22.4 L

10 mL of hydrocarbon, 50 mL of water and 40 mL of carbon dioxide corresponds to 0.45, 2.23,  and 1.79 moles respectively.

Hence, the combustion of 1 mole of hydrocarbon will produce 5 moles of water and 4 moles of carbon dioxide.

Thus, the molecular formula contains 4 carbon atoms and 10 hydrogen atoms.

The hydrocarbon is C4H10 and the combustion reaction is 

C4H10+6.5O2→4CO2+5H2O

x+y=4+10=14

Hence the Total number of carbon and hydrogen atoms in the hydrocarbon is 14.

solution:
Molarity (M) = wtmwt × V × 1000

Using this formula, we can calculate the molarity of the oxalic acid solution as follows:

  1. Mass of hydrated oxalic acid = 3.1500 g
  2. Molar mass of hydrated oxalic acid(H2C2O4·2H2O) = 126.064 g/mol
  3. Volume of the solution = 250.0 mL

Molarity (M)=3.15×1000126×250=0.10

So both assertion land reason are correct.

A + 2 B + 3 C → AB 2 C 3 6 g 6 × 1 0 2 3 atoms 0.036 moles Moles 6 6 0 6 × 1 0 2 3 6 × 1 0 2 3 0.1 mole 1.0 mole 0.036 moles

0.11 ,1.02 ,0.0363; the least value is 0.012, i.e., C is the limiting reagent

subject: Chemistry
Chemistry
options: chemical reactions involve reorganization of atoms. These are neither created nor destroyed in a chemical reaction.
all the atoms of a given element have identical properties including identical mass. Atoms of different elements differ in mass.
when gases combine or reproduced in a chemical reaction they do so in a simple ratio by volume provided all gases are at the same T & P.
solution: When gases combine or reproduced in a chemical reaction they do so in a simple ratio by volume provided all gases are at the same T & P.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Let the density of solution be ' $\mathrm{d}$ '
Molarity of solution given $=3.6$
i.e. $1$ litre of solution contains $3.6$ moles of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$
or $1$ litre of solution contains $3.6 \times 98 \mathrm{~gms}$ of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$
Since, the solution is $29 \%$ by mass.
$100 \mathrm{~gm}$ solution contains $29 \mathrm{~gm} \mathrm{~H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ 
$\frac{100}{\mathrm{~d}} \mathrm{ml}$ solution contains $29 \mathrm{~gm}$ of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ 
$1000 \mathrm{~ml}$ solution contains $3.6 \times 98 \mathrm{~gm}$ of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ $\therefore 3.6 \times 98=\frac{29 \times \mathrm{d}}{100} \times 1000$ 
$d=1.22$
Hence, (C) is correct.
